description: baseline ctx-redesign: 8 4clojure instances on glm-5.1, default ctx prompt with :tree + :defs + REPL trailer

asi.hypothesis: ctx-first design with :tree + multi-fence merge is the current baseline
asi.losses: 1 empty solution (model gave up) + 1 wrong answer; one solve took 57s+
asi.next_action_hint: inspect the failed instance trace to learn what tripped the model

description: drop foundation RLM TACTICS duplicate (replaced by 3-line RULES block); add explicit 'execute side effects in own iteration, done in NEXT' rule

asi.hypothesis: Foundation RLM tactics duplicated engine prompt and referenced retired surfaces (live-vars, journal); shrinking it + explicit side-effect-then-done rule directly fixes the prob1 failure pattern
asi.deltas: prompt body removed ~80 lines RLM TACTICS duplicate; tests updated to assert 'RULES' present
asi.next_action_hint: expand subset to BENCH_INSTANCES=16 or run again to confirm stability

description: confirm slim prompt at 16-instance sample; same code as #2, larger sample for stability check

asi.hypothesis: 8-instance 100% might be sample luck; 16-instance shows true rate
asi.wins: empty_solutions stays 0 (down from 1 in baseline) so the side-effects rule eliminates the 'done without write' bug class; median_seconds 20.5 much faster than baseline 57.1
asi.observation: pass_pct 87.5% same as baseline at smaller sample, but the failure mode changed: baseline failed via empty solution; slim build fails via wrong answer (genuine model mistake, not engine bug)
asi.next_action_hint: shrink AGENTS.md inclusion in foundation runtime context — it dumps ~8KB of vis-specific dev guide that no 4clojure task needs

description: cap AGENTS.md inclusion at 1500 bytes; model reads full via v/cat on demand

asi.hypothesis: AGENTS.md is dev-team-specific; truncating to 1.5KB should not affect 4clojure tasks
asi.result: WORSE: pass_pct 87.5%->75%; tokens slightly UP (model didn't read the truncated file, just got confused?)
asi.rollback_reason: Truncation hurt pass_pct by 12.5pp; 2 errors instead of 1
asi.lesson: AGENTS.md content may carry implicit signal for the model's reasoning quality even on unrelated tasks; or the truncation message itself was distracting
asi.next_action_hint: try OMITTING project-guidance entirely (still expose path) vs truncating partially — see if model performs better with absence than partial truncation

description: omit project-guidance content entirely; expose only path/bytes + v/cat hint

asi.hypothesis: drop AGENTS.md from prompt; model reads on demand via v/cat
asi.result: WORSE: pass_pct 75% (vs 87.5%), 2 empties returned (rule undone?), tokens 91k->77k saved but cost too high
asi.rollback_reason: user direction: AGENTS.md must always be read fully; also pass_pct regressed
asi.lesson: AGENTS.md content is load-bearing for model reasoning quality even on dev-unrelated tasks
asi.next_action_hint: revert; inspect which specific 4clojure problems fail in the slim baseline to find a different optimization angle

description: cap REPL-trailer prior-iter form text at 300 chars

asi.hypothesis: shortening prior-iter form text would save tokens without hurting correctness
asi.result: neutral pass_pct (87.5%) but errors went up (1->2); tokens basically flat; median_seconds doubled to 49s
asi.rollback_reason: wrong principle: trailer is the model's feedback loop, truncating it blinds it to its own prior code; bad idea regardless of metric
asi.lesson: do not cut model context to save tokens; trailer carries exactly what the model needs to learn from
asi.next_action_hint: look at engine-side wins that do NOT remove information: drop redundant preserved-thinking replay (we already include code in trailer), or compact ctx :tree representation

description: promote :iteration {:id :position} from inside :conversation to ctx top-level; prompt now lists all four ctx keys explicitly

asi.hypothesis: iteration is per-iteration scope, conversation is per-conversation scope; nesting them confused the contract; promoting :iteration + listing all ctx keys in prompt should help the model find what it needs faster
asi.wins: 100% pass (16/16) up from 87.5% baseline AND from 87.5% prior slim-build at 16; errors 0 (down from 1-2); empties 0; both previously stable failures (#45 list-as-call and #58 missing parens) now PASSED at this run
asi.deltas: ctx now has top-level :conversation :iteration :tree :defs; prompt enumerates them explicitly; tests updated to drop 'EXPLORE WIDE FIRST' assertion against the retired RLM tactics text
asi.next_action_hint: scale to 24 instances to test stability and discover the next failure mode
